title: Charred Energizing Korean Red Pepper Jjigae
description: A hearty, flavorful, and energizing Korean stew originating from Gyeongsang Province, featuring charred red peppers, roasted vegetables, and plant-based ingredients. This jjigae is perfect for a chilly evening or a quick weeknight meal.
instructions: Char the red bell peppers by placing them on a baking sheet, drizzling with sesame oil, and roasting for 20-25 minutes, or until the skin is blistered and charred.
Peel the charred skin off the peppers and cut them into 1-inch pieces.
Heat sesame oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the gochujang, gochugaru, and rice vinegar to the saucepan, and stir to combine.
Add the charred red peppers, soy sauce, and 1 cup of water to the saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5-7 minutes or until the flavors have melded together.
Season the jjigae with salt to taste.
Garnish with sesame seeds and thinly sliced scallions before serving.
